fact: SetMyCareer positions itself as a scientific and data-driven career counselling and career management company for students and working professionals.
agent_use: Use this to introduce the service and explain why assessment plus counsellor interpretation matters.

fact: Primary audience segments include students in classes 8-10, students in classes 11-12, undergraduate students, postgraduate students, parents of these groups, working professionals with 1-10 years of experience, senior professionals with 10+ years of experience, executives with career breaks, principals/teachers, and others.
agent_use: Use these exact profile buckets during intake and booking.

fact: Service buckets include career counselling, instant career tools, psychological counselling, admission assistance, career coaching and mentoring, personality development, school and college programs, interview skills, CV writing, and dream-career discussion.
agent_use: Route client questions to the most relevant service bucket before recommending a booking.

fact: Career counselling navigation includes dedicated flows for 8th, 9th, 10th, 11th, and 12th standard students.
agent_use: For school students, clarify class, stream pressure, subjects liked/disliked, parent expectations, and timeline.

fact: Career counselling navigation includes undergraduates and postgraduates as distinct graduate segments.
agent_use: For UG/PG clients, ask current course, year, skills, internships, interests, and whether the decision is course choice, job domain, higher study, or transition.

fact: Working professional services include career growth and acceleration, career transition, and career restart after a break.
agent_use: For professionals, distinguish acceleration, transition, and restart; do not reduce every case to job search advice.

fact: Instant Career offerings include Stream Selector, Job Domain Selector, and DIY Career.
agent_use: Mention these only as SetMyCareer service routes; verify current price and availability before quoting.

fact: SetMyCareer describes psychometric career tests as research-based tools for measuring potential, passion, behaviour, interests, personality, and abilities in a short duration.
agent_use: When explaining reports, emphasize that scores are signals needing qualified interpretation.

fact: The stated guidance process tries to understand the person from a 360-degree perspective through qualitative discussion and quantitative psychometric testing.
agent_use: Use this as the high-level process summary in client onboarding.

fact: Career counselling flow includes initial assessment, review of results, discussion of suitable career options, education requirements, job prospects, and creation of a personalized action plan.
agent_use: Use these as stages in the client app journey and voice-agent progress updates.

fact: SetMyCareer highlights 15+ years, international assessments, experienced counsellors, personalized 1:1 approach, and clients across the globe as differentiators.
agent_use: Use sparingly as confidence context; avoid sounding like an advertisement.

fact: The website presents Dr. Nandkishore Rathi as a pioneer in career management in India and describes his scientific career planning model as blending psychometric insights with real-world industry dynamics.
agent_use: Use as background when explaining SetMyCareer's methodology; do not impersonate Dr. Rathi.

fact: The website lists phone +91-9108510058 and office location in Koramangala, Bangalore.
agent_use: Use only if the deployment owner confirms this should be surfaced in the client/voice flow.

fact: The website includes a form checkbox stating the user is not looking for job placement from SetMyCareer.
agent_use: Never imply SetMyCareer guarantees job placement. If a user asks for placement, clarify the distinction between counselling and placement.
